pi_items.metadata_value: Use floatPreferencesKey and booleanPreferencesKey for creating preference keys
pi_items.verified_value: floatPreferencesKey and booleanPreferencesKey are correct DataStore Preferences API functions
pi_items.source: Verified via official Android DataStore documentation at https://developer.android.com/reference/kotlin/androidx/datastore/preferences/core/package-summary. These are standard key factory functions in androidx.datastore.preferences.core package.

pi_items.metadata_value: Handle IOException during reads by emitting empty preferences
pi_items.verified_value: Use .catch() Flow operator before .map() to handle IOException and emit emptyPreferences()
pi_items.source: task.json description line 5 specifies this requirement. setup_task.sh lines 238-241 show .catch block structure. Official DataStore documentation confirms this is the correct pattern: https://developer.android.com/codelabs/android-preferences-datastore

pi_items.metadata_value: Use dataStore.data with .map to read preferences
pi_items.verified_value: dataStore.data.catch(...).map(...) is correct pattern for reading with error handling
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 237-245 show playbackSpeed Flow using this pattern. Official DataStore documentation confirms dataStore.data returns a Flow<Preferences> that can be mapped.

pi_items.verified_value: dataStore.edit { } suspend function is correct for writing preferences
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 257-259 show setPlaybackSpeed and setEnableAutoDownload using dataStore.edit() pattern. This is the standard DataStore mutation API.

privileged_info_summary: The implement_datastore_preferences task is a medium-difficulty Android development exercise requiring implementation of a DataStore Preferences-based repository. The task uses synthetic data: a complete Android project structure is created by setup_task.sh at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/PodcastPlayer with package com.example.podcastplayer. The SettingsRepository class must be implemented using androidx.datastore:datastore-preferences dependency (version 1.0.0 or higher, available from official Google Maven repositories). Implementation requires: (1) adding the datastore-preferences dependency to app/build.gradle.kts, (2) creating two preference keys using floatPreferencesKey('playback_speed') and booleanPreferencesKey('enable_auto_download'), (3) implementing playbackSpeed and enableAutoDownload as Flow<T> properties using dataStore.data with .catch() for IOException handling and .map() for value extraction with defaults (1.0f and true respectively), (4) implementing suspending setter functions setPlaybackSpeed() and setEnableAutoDownload() using dataStore.edit(), and (5) passing 4+ unit tests that validate default values and setter operations. The test suite includes 5 test methods: 4 functional tests and 1 placeholder. This is a standard Android architecture pattern teaching Jetpack DataStore as a replacement for deprecated SharedPreferences.
